page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64 and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier status in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, Passmark CPU scores, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance is average to strong, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests falling into the top 25% and middle 50% respectively. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 257.01 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) drives top-tier results in extended instruction tests and image processing.

Given its high compute density and lower memory-to-vCPU ratio, the D96ls_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, as demonstrated by its strong prompt processing speeds across various model sizes.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers high-density compute power with a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in multi-threaded decompression, Passmark CPU tests, and static web serving. It is highly efficient for CPU-bound workloads, including software compilation, web serving, and text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-focused virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-focused VM with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier multi-core performance in Passmark and Geekbench benchmarks
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for external Premium Storage
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based inference
